disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Widespread itchy hives and stomach cramps began roughly 4-5 hours after eating mammalian (red) meat for dinner
trigger_question_examples: When did the hives and cramps start, and what were you doing beforehand?
Had you eaten anything unusual before this started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started a few hours after dinner - I had a steak, and then hours later I woke up itching all over with these welts and awful cramps.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Feels dizzy and light-headed on standing and is unable to stay upright (presyncope)
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel dizzy or light-headed, especially when you stand up?
Have you felt faint or like you might pass out?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah - every time I try to stand I go really dizzy and feel like I might faint, so I have been stuck on the couch.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I am just tired, I think I just need to lie down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reaction is spreading and escalating over time with worsening nausea, cramping and profound fatigue
trigger_question_examples: Are your symptoms getting better, staying the same, or getting worse?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It is definitely getting worse - the cramps and nausea are building and I feel more and more washed out.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No current throat tightness, difficulty swallowing, or noisy breathing
trigger_question_examples: Is there any tightness in your throat, trouble swallowing, or noisy or difficult breathing?
Do you feel any swelling of your lips, tongue or throat?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my throat feels okay so far and I can breathe alright - it is mainly the skin and my stomach and the dizziness.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No injectable adrenaline (adrenaline auto-injector) available in the home
trigger_question_examples: Do you have an adrenaline auto-injector (EpiPen) at home?
Is there any allergy medication or adrenaline pen in the house?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, we do not have anything like an EpiPen here.
